content.problemStatement:
After merging work, your branches start to pile up. \
To keep your repository clean, you should delete branches you no longer need.

**Why Delete a Branch?** \
Because **once a feature is merged**, that branch has served its purpose. \
**Keeping unused branches only creates confusion**.

**Git command to delete :**
```sss
git branch -d branch-name
```
Use this when the branch is **already merged into `master`** or another branch.
**Git will stop you from deleting** if the branch still has **unmerged work**.

---

**Force Delete :**
```sss
git branch -D branch-name
```
This deletes the branch even if it has unmerged changes. \
**Use only when you're sure you don’t need the work on that branch**.
